terms.definition: A contractual obligation where one party agrees to compensate the other for specified losses or damages arising from third party claims.
terms.example: Vendor indemnifies Customer against claims of IP infringement.
terms.legal_effect: Creates obligation to pay legal costs and settlement/judgment amounts

terms.definition: Events beyond reasonable control that prevent a party from performing contractual obligations.
terms.example: Natural disasters, war, terrorism, epidemics, government actions.
terms.legal_effect: Excuses non-performance during event duration

terms.definition: A clause that caps or restricts the amount of damages a party can recover.
terms.example: Liability capped at fees paid in preceding 12 months.
terms.legal_effect: Limits financial exposure

terms.definition: Indirect damages that do not flow directly from breach, such as lost profits or business interruption.
terms.example: Lost revenue due to software downtime.
terms.legal_effect: Often excluded in commercial contracts

terms.definition: Tax withheld by payer from payments to deductee and remitted to government.
terms.example: 2% TDS on works contract payments.
terms.legal_effect: Statutory obligation; failure results in disallowance and penalties

terms.term: TAN - Tax Deduction and Collection Account Number
terms.definition: Unique 10-digit alphanumeric number required for persons deducting or collecting tax.
terms.example: DELR12345F
terms.legal_effect: Mandatory for all deductors; penalty for non-compliance

terms.term: MSME - Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
terms.definition: Enterprises classified based on investment in plant and machinery or equipment.
terms.example: Micro: < Rs. 25 lakhs (manufacturing). Small: < Rs. 5 crores.
terms.legal_effect: Special protections including mandatory 45-day payment terms and compound interest on delays

terms.definition: Comprehensive indirect tax on supply of goods and services in India.
terms.example: CGST + SGST for intra-state supply, IGST for inter-state.
terms.legal_effect: Compliance required for registration, invoicing, filing returns

terms.definition: A promise by the seller about the quality, condition, or performance of goods or services.
terms.example: Software will conform to documentation for 90 days.
terms.legal_effect: Creates remedy for non-conformance

terms.definition: Commitments regarding service performance metrics and remedies for failure.
terms.example: 99.9% uptime, 4-hour response time.
terms.legal_effect: Quantifiable performance standards with remedies

terms.definition: Arrangement where source code is deposited with third party and released upon specified events.
terms.example: Release upon vendor bankruptcy or failure to maintain software.
terms.legal_effect: Provides customer self-help remedy if vendor fails to maintain software

terms.term: DPDP Act 2023 - Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023
terms.definition: India's comprehensive data protection law regulating processing of personal data.
terms.example: Requires consent, breach notification, data retention limits.
terms.legal_effect: Penalties up to Rs. 250 Crore, mandatory compliance obligations

terms.definition: Mechanism where recipient of goods/services is liable to pay GST instead of supplier.
terms.example: Goods Transport Agency services, legal services by advocate.
terms.legal_effect: Recipient must pay GST directly to government

terms.definition: Credit for GST paid on inputs, available to set off against output GST liability.
terms.example: Manufacturer claims ITC on raw materials against GST on finished goods.
terms.legal_effect: Reduces effective tax burden; conditions apply for eligibility

terms.definition: 10-digit alphanumeric identifier for all tax-related transactions.
terms.example: AAAPL1234C
terms.legal_effect: Mandatory for financial transactions over specified thresholds

terms.definition: Legal doctrine where employer or hiring party is considered author/owner of created work.
terms.example: Software developed by consultant for company as work for hire.
terms.legal_effect: Hiring party owns IP automatically; no separate assignment needed

terms.definition: Intellectual property owned by a party before entering into an agreement.
terms.example: Vendor's pre-existing software libraries used in development.
terms.legal_effect: Remains owned by original party; license granted to other party

terms.definition: Intellectual property created specifically under an agreement.
terms.example: Custom software developed for customer.
terms.legal_effect: Ownership determined by agreement (often customer)

terms.definition: Pre-determined damages amount for specified breach, agreed by parties.
terms.example: Rs. 10,000 per day for delay beyond completion date.
terms.legal_effect: Enforceable if reasonable estimate, not penalty

terms.definition: Failure to perform any contractual obligation without legal excuse.
terms.example: Non-payment, failure to deliver, defective performance.
terms.legal_effect: Non-breaching party entitled to remedies

terms.definition: Time allowed to remedy a contractual breach before termination or other action.
terms.example: 30 days to cure payment default.
terms.legal_effect: Prevents immediate termination for curable breaches

terms.definition: Third party engaged by data processor to process personal data.
terms.example: Cloud hosting provider engaged by SaaS vendor.
terms.legal_effect: Requires written consent and flow-down of data protection obligations
